_note: All of Adam & Eve's children (Genesis 4-5): Cain, Abel (already had Seth), plus the note that they had 'other sons and daughters' (Gen 5:4). Cain's line runs Cain -> Enoch -> Irad -> Mehujael -> Methushael -> Lamech (a DIFFERENT Enoch and Lamech than Seth's line) -> Jabal, Jubal, Tubal-cain, Naamah (Gen 4:17-22). Cain's whole line perished in the Flood. Appended to the existing Adam & Eve union. Distinct ids used so the two Enochs / two Lamechs don't collide.

children.label:
Cain
c.3990 BC · Adam's firstborn; slew Abel · Genesis 4

Abel
c.3980 BC · the shepherd, killed by Cain · Genesis 4

Awan
c.3980 BC · daughter of Adam & Eve; Cain's sister & wife
· named in the Book of Jubilees 4:9 (unnamed in Genesis)

and other sons & daughters
Genesis 5:4 · the Life of Adam & Eve counts 30 sons & 30 daughters

Enoch
son of Cain; a city bore his name · Genesis 4:17
(not Enoch who 'walked with God')

Lamech
c.3830 BC · of Cain's line; took two wives (Adah & Zillah)
spoke the 'Song of the Sword' — 'Lamech seventy-sevenfold' (Gen 4:23-24),
the first poem in scripture · in legend, blind Lamech accidentally slew Cain (Jasher, midrash)
(not Lamech father of Noah)

Jabal
father of those who dwell in tents & keep herds · Genesis 4:20

Jubal
father of all who play the harp & flute · Genesis 4:21

Tubal-cain
forger of every tool of bronze & iron; father of metalworking · Genesis 4:22
· in legend, guided blind Lamech's fatal arrow at Cain

Naamah
daughter of Zillah; sister of Tubal-cain · Genesis 4:22
· in rabbinic tradition (Gen. Rabbah 23:3) she is Noah's wife
